title: Round barns in Illinois

infobox.value: MPL001 – Round Barns in Illinois Thematic Resources

were the subject of a [multiple property submission](/wiki/Multiple_property_submission "Multiple property submission") to the National Register of Historic Places in the U.S. state of [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"). The submission consists of 18 Illinois [round barns](/wiki/Round_barns "Round barns") located throughout the state. The list had major additions in 1982 and 1984. In 1983, 1992 and 2003 one property was added to the submission and in 1994 a [historic district](/wiki/Historic_district "Historic district") at the [University of Illinois](/wiki/University_of_Illinois_at_Urbana%E2%80%93Champaign "University of Illinois at Urbana–Champaign"), including three round barns, was added to the submission and the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places"). The highest concentration of round barns on the submission occurs in [Stephenson County](/wiki/Stephenson_County,_Illinois "Stephenson County, Illinois"). Five Stephenson County round barns were added to the National Register on February 23, 1984.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

United States historic place

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Round Barns in Illinois TR | |
| [U.S. National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") | |
|  | |
| This architectural drawing is of one of three University of Illinois round barns. | |
| Location | [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"), United States |
| Architectural style | [Round barns](/wiki/Round_barns "Round barns") |
| [MPS](/wiki/Multiple_Property_Submission "Multiple Property Submission") | MPL001 – Round Barns in Illinois Thematic Resources |
| NRHP reference No. | [64000152](https://npgallery.nps.gov/AssetDetail/NRIS/64000152),1982: 82000401, 82002536, 82002582, 82002586. 1983: 83000326. 1984: 84001150, 84001152, 84001155, 84001157, 84001164. 1992: 92001017. 1994: 94000030. 2003: 02000750. |
| Added to NRHP | August 26, 1982; December 7, 1982; 1983; February 23, 1984; 1992; 1994; 2003.[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1) |

**Round barns in Illinois** were the subject of a [multiple property submission](/wiki/Multiple_property_submission "Multiple property submission") to the National Register of Historic Places in the U.S. state of [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"). The submission consists of 18 Illinois [round barns](/wiki/Round_barns "Round barns") located throughout the state. The list had major additions in 1982 and 1984. In 1983, 1992 and 2003 one property was added to the submission and in 1994 a [historic district](/wiki/Historic_district "Historic district") at the [University of Illinois](/wiki/University_of_Illinois_at_Urbana%E2%80%93Champaign "University of Illinois at Urbana–Champaign"), including three round barns, was added to the submission and the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places"). The highest concentration of round barns on the submission occurs in [Stephenson County](/wiki/Stephenson_County,_Illinois "Stephenson County, Illinois"). Five Stephenson County round barns were added to the National Register on February 23, 1984.

## Significance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Significance")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/UofIRoundBarns.jpg/250px-UofIRoundBarns.jpg)](/wiki/File:UofIRoundBarns.jpg)

One of the barns on the University of Illinois' campus at sunset

Illinois, as home to the [Agricultural Experiment Station](/wiki/Agricultural_Experiment_Station "Agricultural Experiment Station") at the University of Illinois, played a key role in promoting and popularizing the round barn design. The three round barns that are now part of the historic district at the experiment station helped influence Illinoisians and, in turn, other Midwesterners, to build round barns. In the case of the Raymond Schulz Round Barn, near [Pontiac, Illinois](/wiki/Pontiac,_Illinois "Pontiac, Illinois"), it was constructed specifically because its owner had viewed the round barns at the university.[[2]](#cite_note-ray-2)

The round barn was the last of five types of American barns to be built by [carpenters](/wiki/Carpenter "Carpenter"), the others being the [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_barn "Dutch barn"), [English](/wiki/English_barn "English barn"), [connected](/wiki/Connected_barn "Connected barn") and [Pennsylvania barns](/wiki/Pennsylvania_barn "Pennsylvania barn").[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3) Collectively, they represent the foray of science into the field of agriculture.[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3)

## Barns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Barns")]

The round barn multiple property submission (MPS) in Illinois includes 18 round barns.[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3)

### Original submission

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Original submission")]

Seven round barns were added to the National Register per the original MPS in 1982. At the time, just one other Illinois round barn was listed on the Register, the [Ryan Round Barn](/wiki/Ryan_Round_Barn "Ryan Round Barn") in [Henry County](/wiki/Henry_County,_Illinois "Henry County, Illinois").[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3) Three of the 1982 round barns have since been removed from the Register, most likely due to demolition or destruction, the [John McCarthy Round Barn](/w/index.php?title=John_McCarthy_Round_Barn&action=edit&redlink=1 "John McCarthy Round Barn (page does not exist)"), the [Clyde Leek Round Barn](/w/index.php?title=Clyde_Leek_Round_Barn&action=edit&redlink=1 "Clyde Leek Round Barn (page does not exist)") and the [Clarence Forehand Round Barn](/w/index.php?title=Clarence_Forehand_Round_Barn&action=edit&redlink=1 "Clarence Forehand Round Barn (page does not exist)") were removed from the listing in December 1995.[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3)[[4]](#cite_note-nrhplist-4) The other barns listed in 1982 were: the [Raymond Schulz Round Barn](/wiki/Raymond_Schulz_Round_Barn "Raymond Schulz Round Barn"), [Virginia Tillery Round Barn](/wiki/Virginia_Tillery_Round_Barn "Virginia Tillery Round Barn"), [Clarence Kleinkopf Round Barn](/wiki/Clarence_Kleinkopf_Round_Barn "Clarence Kleinkopf Round Barn") and, in December, the [Ron George Round Barn](/wiki/Ron_George_Round_Barn "Ron George Round Barn") in [Will County](/wiki/Will_County,_Illinois "Will County, Illinois"). The other three were added to the Register on August 26, 1982.[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3) An eighth round barn was determined eligible and nominated to the Register between December 1982 and February 1983, but the owner of the [Willard White Round Barn](/w/index.php?title=Willard_White_Round_Barn&action=edit&redlink=1 "Willard White Round Barn (page does not exist)") in [DeKalb County](/wiki/DeKalb_County,_Illinois "DeKalb County, Illinois"), near Hinckley, objected to the nomination, and the barn was not listed.[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3)

### Lewis Round Barn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Lewis Round Barn")]

Main article: [Lewis Round Barn](/wiki/Lewis_Round_Barn "Lewis Round Barn")

The Lewis Round Barn is located in [Mendon, Illinois](/wiki/Mendon,_Illinois "Mendon, Illinois"). The barn has been restored and relocated and is the latest addition to the Round Barn MPS. It was added to the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 2003.

### Stephenson County area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Stephenson County area")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/Freeport_Il_Bruce_Round_Barn7.JPG/250px-Freeport_Il_Bruce_Round_Barn7.JPG)](/wiki/File:Freeport_Il_Bruce_Round_Barn7.JPG)

The [James Bruce Round Barn](/wiki/James_Bruce_Round_Barn "James Bruce Round Barn") near [Freeport, Illinois](/wiki/Freeport,_Illinois "Freeport, Illinois")

At the time of the 1984 addition to the Round Barn MPS [Stephenson County, Illinois](/wiki/Stephenson_County,_Illinois "Stephenson County, Illinois") had more round barns than any other county in the United States.[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3) Of the 31 round barns constructed in the four county area that makes up the Stephenson County area for the purpose of the MPS, 21 are found within the borders of Stephenson County. The other ten are spread out over [Winnebago County, Illinois](/wiki/Winnebago_County,_Illinois "Winnebago County, Illinois"), [Green County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Green_County,_Wisconsin "Green County, Wisconsin") and [Rock County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Rock_County,_Wisconsin "Rock County, Wisconsin"). The 21 barns in the county exceeds the 18 round barns in [Vernon County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Vernon_County,_Wisconsin "Vernon County, Wisconsin") and the 12 round barns in [Fulton County, Indiana](/wiki/Fulton_County,_Indiana "Fulton County, Indiana").[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3) On the original January 1984 nomination seven round barns were included in the Stephenson County area, of those six were added to the MPS and, thus, the National Register of Historic Places. As of 2007 five of the six round barns listed in 1984 are still on the Register. They are the [Dennis Otte Round Barn](/wiki/Dennis_Otte_Round_Barn "Dennis Otte Round Barn"), the [Gerald Harbach Round Barn](/wiki/Gerald_Harbach_Round_Barn "Gerald Harbach Round Barn"), [James Bruce Round Barn](/wiki/James_Bruce_Round_Barn "James Bruce Round Barn"), [Chris Jensen Round Barn](/wiki/Chris_Jensen_Round_Barn "Chris Jensen Round Barn") and the [Charles Fehr Round Barn](/wiki/Charles_Fehr_Round_Barn "Charles Fehr Round Barn").[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3) The [Robert Weber Round Barn](/wiki/Robert_Weber_Round_Barn "Robert Weber Round Barn") is in nearby Winnebago County.

### Robert Buckles barn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Robert Buckles barn")]

The [Buckles Round Barn](/wiki/Robert_Buckles_Barn "Robert Buckles Barn"), located in [Logan County](/wiki/Logan_County,_Illinois "Logan County, Illinois") near [Mount Pulaski](/wiki/Mount_Pulaski,_Illinois "Mount Pulaski, Illinois"), was added to the National Register of Historic Places, and the Round Barn MPS, on February 10, 1983. Its roof was damaged after a 1927 tornado and remained damaged until a $30,000 repair and restoration was undertaken in 1982. This round barn was built in 1917.[[5]](#cite_note-buckles-5)

### University of Illinois

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: University of Illinois")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/50/To_scale_cutaway_U_of_I_round_barn.jpg/250px-To_scale_cutaway_U_of_I_round_barn.jpg)](/wiki/File:To_scale_cutaway_U_of_I_round_barn.jpg)

A [to-scale](/wiki/Scale_(ratio) "Scale (ratio)") cutaway of one of the U of I barns, note the center [silo](/wiki/Storage_silo "Storage silo")

Main article: [University of Illinois round barns](/wiki/University_of_Illinois_round_barns "University of Illinois round barns")

Part of the 1994 National Register of Historic Places listing for the [University of Illinois Experimental Dairy Farm Historic District](/wiki/University_of_Illinois_Experimental_Dairy_Farm_Historic_District "University of Illinois Experimental Dairy Farm Historic District") includes three [round barns](/wiki/Round_barns "Round barns"). The barns were constructed every two years from 1908 thru 1912.[[6]](#cite_note-uofi-6) The barns are known by the simple designations, Barn #1, Barn #2 and Building #857. Though all round barns, Building #857 includes a rectangular brick wing attached to main round section of the building.[[6]](#cite_note-uofi-6)

### Wheeler-Magnus Round Barn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Wheeler-Magnus Round Barn")]

The [Wheeler-Magnus Round Barn](/wiki/Wheeler-Magnus_Round_Barn "Wheeler-Magnus Round Barn") is the only round barn listed on the National Register of Historic Places located in [Cook County](/wiki/Cook_County,_Illinois "Cook County, Illinois"). The barn was added to both the Round Barn MPS and the Register in 1992.[[3]](#cite_note-mps-3)

## Other Illinois round barns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Other Illinois round barns")]

There are, certainly, dozens of additional round barns standing throughout Illinois. Of the round barns listed on the National Register of Historic Places just one was listed before the development of the MPS, the Ryan Round Barn north of Kewanee.[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of round barns#Illinois](/wiki/List_of_round_barns#Illinois "List of round barns"), a tabulation of all known Illinois round barns

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Round_barns_in_Illinois&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: References")]

[National Park Service](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service"). July 9, 2010.

Retrieved 8 February 2007.
